Thank you

for supporting a better future for Binti!

See your pledge at https://binti.tz/pledge/
×

Pledge for Better

  • Never participate in any marriage ceremony for someone below the age of 18 years
  • Educate my friends, family, and community about the harmful effects of child marriage
  • Report any child marriage event or discussion happening around me to the relevant authorities, and /or my parents, my teachers, my faith leader and others adults
  • Push for implementation of the judicial decision that increased the minimum age of marriage for both boys and girls in Tanzania to 18 years
  • Collaborate with other concerned citizens and relevant authorities to stop child marriage in Tanzania
Disclaimer: The information shared by you is encrypted, and anonymised for pledge counting purposes only. Your information will not be sold, rented or leased to any third party individual, government agency, or company at any time.
×

MEET BINTI

Hi, My name is Binti.

I am 14 years old, and live in Tanzania, I go to school and I want to go to university one day. My dream is to be an Engineer and build roads and bridges across my beautiful country and beyond, across Africa.

My dream will only come true if my parents let me finish school and help me grow and be everything I am meant to be.

Child marriage is still too common in Tanzania and it destroys the dreams of so many bintis. Join me to raise awareness to help the laws in our land change for better.

For every girl like me

Talk Soon,

Binti

Why this Campaign

Child marriage deprives bintis of education, opportunities and access to health and protection. It keeps them in poverty, and makes them vulnerable to domestic violence and sexually transmitted infections. Child marriage increases the risk of deaths to the underage mother and her baby.

  • What is the Need?

  • Why Now?

  • What to do?

  • Did you know?

Why #EndChildMarriage in Tanzania?

  • Child marriage ends childhood and negatively impacts childrens rights to education, health, and protection.
  • Children married off before the age of 18 are more likely to drop out of school and lack the skills to earn money to support their families or contribute to the community.
  • Child brides are exposed to a higher likelihood of domestic violence and sexually transmitted infections, including HIV & AIDS.
  • Child brides give birth to children with a higher risk of dying due to complications during pregnancy and childbirth.
  • Child marriage negatively impacts the Tanzanian economy and creates even more pathways to an inter-generational cycle of poverty.

Why Now?

  • Child marriage ends childhood and negatively impacts childrens rights to education, health, and protection.
  • Children married off before the age of 18 are more likely to drop out of school and lack the skills to earn money to support their families or contribute to the community.
  • Child brides are exposed to a higher likelihood of domestic violence and sexually transmitted infections, including HIV & AIDS.
  • Child brides give birth to children with a higher risk of dying due to complications during pregnancy and childbirth.

What to do?

  • Join the #EndChildMarriage awareness campaign: Sign the pledge at binti.tz!
  • Talk to your family and friends to make a commitment not to participate in a child marriage and then sign the pledge.
  • Discuss the issue in your school/work/religious and youth groups or other social spaces you belong to online and offline.
  • Ask your traditional and religious leader or head of your association or institution to join the campaign and speak up against child marriage using their own platforms .
  • Report cases of child marriage in your communities to the appropriate authorities.

Did you know?

  • Tanzania ranks 11th in the world for the number of child marriages.
  • 6,500 girls dropped out of school in Tanzania in 2020 alone due to pregnancy.
  • More than 3 out of 10 young women in Tanzania got married when they were children.
  • The recent judgment by both the High and Appeal courts made it illegal to give girls under 18 years of age out in marriage.

Bintis of Tanzania – We hear you !

Stories of courage

Binti

Do not let my story repeat

#SuperDadas wangu 🤩🤩 wako hapa na ujumbe muhimu: "Kama tungeolewa tukiwa watoto, tusingelikua hapa tulipo LEO!!" Wanasimama🙌🏾 Wanazugumza🗣 Je Unasikiliza?? Ni wakati wa #TokomezaNdoaZaUtotoni kwa watoto wakike nchini 🇹🇿 Tembelea www.binti.tz!

Posted by Binti on Friday, July 29, 2022


"Kuna athari mbaya sio tu kwa mtoto wa kike na sio kwa wanawake tu, bali kwa taifa. Watanzania wenzangu, hili ni jukumu la kila mmoja kutekeleza jambo hili, si mtu mmoja tu. Kwa pamoja tutakomesha ndoa za utotoni!" - Askofu Dk.Stanley Hotayi, wa Kanisa la Anglikana, Dayosisi ya Arusha na Makamu wa Kwanza wa Rais wa Baraza la Kikristo Tanzania Saini ahadi yako leo 👉🏾www.binti.tz 💪🏾

Posted by Binti on Thursday, August 4, 2022

Wapendwa wazazi wetu, Mnadeni kwa kila mtoto wa Kitanzania, bila kujali jinsia. Wasichana wanapaswa kucheza na marafiki zao🤗, kumaliza shule 📚na kujitengenezea jina katika taifa letu🇹🇿 bila hofu ya ndoa za utotoni! Asante sana Pr.Mack Malekana, Askofu mkuu wa Kanisa la Waadventista wa Sabato, kusimama nami! Weka ahadi yako leo kutokomeza ndoa za utotoni 👉🏾www.binti.tz

Posted by Binti on Monday, August 8, 2022

Sikilizeni dada zangu🗣❤️ Wasichana hukosa elimu na nafasi ya kuamua maisha yao ya baadaye pale wanapo olewa mapema! Wewe pia unaweza kukomesha swala la ndoa za utotoni! Saini ahadi yako 👉🏾 www.binti.tz SASA🙌🏾

Posted by Binti on Tuesday, August 9, 2022

Wasichana ni watetezi wa haki zao✊🏾Ila tunahitaji ushirikiano wa kaka zetu pia. Ewe, kaka! Je, unasimama nasi? Onyesha umoja wako kwa kusaini ahadi ya kutokomeza ndoa za utotoni 👉🏾 www.binti.tz Vita ni ya sote pamoja!!! Kaka yangu Emmanuel Cosmas ashaweka ahadi yake! Baado wewe tuu💪🏾 #NilindeKwanza #TokomezaNdoaZaUtotoni #Binti #GirlsNotBrides

Posted by Binti on Thursday, August 18, 2022

Watoto wenzangu OYEEEEE 🤩🤩🤩 Wanafunzi hawa kutoka Mungano Primary wananipa furaha kama yote 🥰 Wasichana wanapaswa kucheza na marafiki zao, kumaliza shule na kujitengenezea jina katika taifa letu bila hofu ya ndoa za utotoni! Weka ahadi yako leo kutokomeza ndoa za utotoni 👉🏾www.binti.tz

Posted by Binti on Thursday, September 8, 2022

Get Support

Are you trying to protect yourself or a binti from child marriage?

You are not alone.

Reach out to us:

Child Helpline

Police

Community Organizations

Partners

We are working to build a healthier, brighter and better future for every Binti of Tanzania.

Binti is the mascot for the #EndChildMarriage campaign, which is an initiative by UNICEF Tanzania to raise awareness about the far reaching negative impacts of child marriages on girls, and by extension, the whole nation. Take the pledge to support this campaign.
